sections.paragraph:
χορεύω (χορός) impf. ἐχόρευον; aor. ptc. fem. χορεύσασα GJs (Trag. et al.; LXX, Philo; Jos., Ant. 17, 235; Mel., P. 80, 592) dance in chorus Hs 9, 11, 5 (Leutzsch, Hermas 488, n. 322). In front of angels GJs 15:3; cp. Coll. Alex. p. 198 no. 36, 19 for Isis.—B. 689. DELG s.v. χορός.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Leutzsch, Hermas Leutzsch, Hermas = MLeutzsch, Hirt des Hermas—Lists 1 (s. H), 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1087
